title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria 'Tori' Amore, 17, went missing from her home in Waltham on July 3, police say.
Police report Amore was last seen in a dark sedan and left without an EpiPen or inhaler.
Authorities request public assistance and provided a contact number for information on Amore's whereabouts.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth, officials stated.
The bicyclist was pronounced dead at the scene, and the truck driver cooperated with police investigations.
Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police are jointly investigating the crash; no charges have been announced.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston man Patrick Donovan charged with multiple offenses after alleged assault at East Boston Burger King.
Donovan accused of punching a customer, attacking an employee, and resisting arrest at MBTA Maverick Station.
Donovan pleaded not guilty, released on recognizance, and commanded to avoid Maverick Square and Burger King.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, charged with murder and firearm-related offenses in the death of Xavier Bautista, a DPW worker.
Bautista, 32, was shot near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge on July 4; he was found an hour later.
Cambridge debates gunshot detection technology's effectiveness after the shooting, with increased police patrols in response.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died in a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts.
Authorities reported a sudden wind gust interfered with his parachute, causing a 30-foot fall.
This is the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving within a year, with investigations ongoing.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's request to allow testimony from other women with postpartum mental illness was denied by Judge William Sullivan.
Jury selection for Lindsay Clancy's trial on three first-degree murder charges begins July 20, expected to last 4 to 6 weeks.
Defense plans an insanity plea, citing Clancy's alleged postpartum mental illness, while prosecutors argue she acted with intent.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranked No. 8 in ESPN's latest NFL top-10 quarterbacks list, after being unranked last season.
Maye led the NFL in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage, joining Tom Brady in achieving this.
Patriots improved to a 14-3 record and reached the Super Bowl with Maye, despite a previous 4-13 season.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship in Seabrook, N.H., eating 7.77 pounds of wings.
Webb defeated 18-time Nathan's Hot Dog champion Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds, and Massachusetts native Geoff Esper.
James Webb received $2,500 for his victory, which highlighted Kowloon's iconic Saugus Wings.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' linebackers, led by Harold Landry, were among the NFL's top-rated units last season.
The team finished eighth in the league for yards allowed per game and fourth for points allowed per game.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, creating uncertainty in the lineup ahead of the 2026 season.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph, on Sunday afternoon, per the Norfolk District Attorney’s Office.
Randolph police and fire departments responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at the pond around 4 p.m.
State Police are investigating the incident; the man was found by firefighters and declared dead at a hospital.
